core_mechanism: Resonance-tuned crossover of a control parameter (from repulsive to attractive effective coupling) that stabilizes a metastable, locally-stable collective state which is not the global energy minimum, sustained against relaxation by strong correlations acting as an effective rigidity (stiffness) barrier rather than by being the lowest-energy configuration
brief_reason: The abstract stabilizes a highly excited (non-ground) many-body phase under attractive interactions by strengthening correlations across a confinement-induced resonance and diagnoses the repulsive-to-attractive crossover via stiffness and energy, which is a metastable state held by a correlation-induced rigidity reached by tuning a coupling through a resonance.
